# Onboarding: Prompt Authoring

Welcome to the prompt-authoring skill! This guide will help you write, structure, and maintain high-quality Copilot prompt files.

## Step-by-step Guide

1. Read [SKILL.md](SKILL.md) for core authoring patterns and procedures.

2. Review [examples/README.md](examples/README.md) for practical prompt authoring patterns.

3. Define your prompt's goal, required inputs, and expected outputs.

4. Choose the right agent and reference any required skills.

5. Write the prompt using the recommended structure and output format.

6. Test your prompt with realistic inputs and review the output.

7. Update routing and smoke tests if you add or change a prompt.

## Reviewing and Testing Prompts

- Use the self-check in [SKILL.md](SKILL.md) before finalising.

- Test with edge cases and typical user inputs.

- Review output for determinism and clarity.

## Resources

- [SKILL.md](SKILL.md): Full procedure and best practices.

- [examples/README.md](examples/README.md): Copy/paste-ready prompt patterns.

- [prompt-engineering/SKILL.md](../prompt-engineering/SKILL.md): Prompt design and integration.

- [agent-authoring/SKILL.md](../agent-authoring/SKILL.md): Agent design.
